title: tylecodon buchholzianus
intro: Tylecodon buchholzianus, also known as the Buchholz's tylecodon or the 'living stone', is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae, characterized by its thick, stone-like leaves that help it store water in arid environments. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Tylecodon buchholzianus include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and crushed to make a tea or powder that can be used to treat various ailments.
